prompt: {"task":"editorial website hero design reference - the work changing state, manual chaos to ordered output","output":"single 16:9 horizontal image, photographic editorial quality, film still feel, subtle 35mm grain at 7 percent, no watermark, no signature","hard_constraints":["DO NOT use a conventional website hero layout","NO before/after split screen with vertical divider, NO diptych - this must read as ONE continuous photograph of a desk in a single moment, the transition is implied by the layout of papers across the surface","NO robot hand, NO mechanical arm, NO holographic overlay, NO glow, NO neon, NO neural-network graphic, NO Powered-by-GPT chip","NO Accenture/Deloitte stock-photo grid look","NO human, NO person, NO hand, NO face, NO head visible anywhere in the photograph","Treat this like an overhead still life from Monocle or Cereal magazine. The composition reads in three horizontal zones across the frame.","focal_point":"OVERHEAD top-down view of a heavy wooden desk in a strategy room. The desk fills the lower two-thirds of the frame; the upper third is dark navy plaster wall (negative space for the headline). LEFT THIRD of the desk: a messy scattered pile of handwritten intake forms, yellow sticky notes (one reading 'TRIAGE' in marker), open envelopes with letters pulled half out, a coffee-stained scrap of paper, a paperclip loose on the wood. CENTER of the desk: a single page in mid-transit, half-resting on the wood, the upper half of it ALREADY rubber-stamped 'PROCESSED' in dark red ink with today's date, the lower half still showing raw handwritten notes. RIGHT THIRD of the desk: the same kind of papers but ORDERED, neatly squared into a stack with a brass paperclip, top page stamped 'PROCESSED' and tabbed with a single colored sticky flag, a small leather notebook closed and squared to the stack. A black fountain pen rests at a precise angle between the center and right zones.
